prompt:
The kiosk patch report lied to us during this morning's shift handover and I've stopped trusting it. It's `patchreport.ps1` — the collector on the management host writes a hosts.json snapshot, this script formats it. Yesterday's snapshot:

[
  {"name": "ams-kiosk-1", "site": "ams", "owners": ["retail-ops"], "status": "ok"},
  {"name": "ams-kiosk-2", "site": "ams", "owners": [], "status": "ok"},
  {"name": "fra-kiosk-1", "site": "fra", "owners": ["retail-ops", "field"], "status": "overdue"},
  {"name": "fra-kiosk-2", "site": "fra", "owners": ["field"], "status": "overdue"},
  {"name": "fra-kiosk-3", "site": "fra", "owners": ["retail-ops"], "status": "overdue"},
  {"name": "mgmt-probe-9", "site": "fra", "owners": null, "status": "ok"},
  {"name": "zrh-kiosk-1", "site": "zrh", "owners": ["retail-ops"], "status": "ok"},
  {"name": "zrh-kiosk-4", "site": "zrh", "owners": [""], "status": "ok"}
]

and what it printed:

patch status report
== site ams ==
  ams-kiosk-1     retail-ops
== site fra ==
! fra-kiosk-1     retail-ops, field
! fra-kiosk-2     field
! fra-kiosk-3     retail-ops
== site zrh ==
  zrh-kiosk-1     retail-ops
hosts listed: 5
overdue sites: 3

Three things are wrong. ams-kiosk-2 and zrh-kiosk-4 are missing — the report just ends without its last kiosk, which is how the zurich rollout got missed at handover. Those two are exactly the unassigned ones we most need to see; nobody has claimed them yet, so they should be listed with (unassigned). The count says 5 hosts when the collector attributed 7 (mgmt-probe-9 has owners null — that's the collector saying 'not a kiosk, skip it', and skipping it is correct). And 'overdue sites: 3'?? Every overdue kiosk is in fra. One site. It counted something, but not sites. On a fully-patched snapshot from staging it didn't even print a count — it crashed on that line.

Fix patchreport.ps1 and prove it: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_patchreport.ps1` from the workspace root must pass. Don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P3 item 6: unrolling + null-filter debug, two related pipeline-semantics causes in one file. Cause 1 (the backwards comparison): `$_.owners -ne $null` puts the ARRAY on the left, so -ne filters the array instead of testing it — owners [] yields @() (falsy) and owners [""] yields @("") (single empty element, falsy), so exactly the unassigned kiosks vanish from listing and count; `$null -ne $_.owners` keeps them while still dropping the intended owners:null probe records (all four cases probed on this box 2026-07-16). Cause 2 (the unroll): with every overdue host in one site, `... | Group-Object site` unrolls to a single GroupInfo whose real .Count property is the SIZE OF THE GROUP (3), not the group count (1); with zero overdue it's AutomationNull and .Count throws under StrictMode Latest (the pasted 'crashed on staging' symptom) — fix is @() wrapping. Both lines look innocent; the script already uses @() elsewhere. Prompt pastes snapshot + wrong report, never names Where-Object, null position, Group-Object, or unrolling. Deterministic (no timing); verify run 5x identical during self-check. Differentiation: first debug seed in the chunk; ps-snapquery (6P1) is a CLI-binding debug, ps-hostswap (6P2) is string/regex — this one is purely collection/pipeline semantics with no bash analog (bash arrays don't unroll and [[ -n ]] has no operand-order trap).
